title: Minor burn review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports escalation for face or neck and airway involvement, inhalation, chemical or electrical burns, circumferential or deep burns, large surface area, special sites, comorbidity, infection, or associated trauma.
red_flags.rationale: Relevant to clinician-assessed concern and referral status without assigning urgency.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports escalation for face or neck and airway involvement, inhalation, chemical or electrical burns, circumferential or deep burns, large surface area, special sites, comorbidity, infection, or associated trauma.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Burns

examination.text: Supports airway and inhalation assessment, neurological and neurovascular context, burn site, size or area, depth, blistering, circumferential features, and total-body-surface-area estimation.
examination.rationale: Relevant to recording clinician-assessed site, depth, size, airway and observations without calculating TBSA.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports airway and inhalation assessment, neurological and neurovascular context, burn site, size or area, depth, blistering, circumferential features, and total-body-surface-area estimation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Burns

exact_section.heading: Section 7 — airway, vital signs, depth, site, size, and total body surface area

assessment.text: Supports thermal, scald, electrical, chemical, friction, depth, blistering, redness, pain, site, and mechanism documentation.
assessment.rationale: Relevant to clinician-entered mechanism and classification only when directly assessed; no classification is inferred.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports thermal, scald, electrical, chemical, friction, depth, blistering, redness, pain, site, and mechanism documentation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Burns

referral.text: Supports clinician-documented burn-care discussion, dressing, analgesia, tetanus, return precautions, infection monitoring, arranged follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment or dose selection.
referral.rationale: Relevant to documenting care and follow-up actually decided by the clinician; no plan is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented burn-care discussion, dressing, analgesia, tetanus, return precautions, infection monitoring, arranged follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment or dose selection.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Burns

exact_section.heading: Sections 8–9 — clinician-led burn care, dressing, analgesia, tetanus, monitoring, and follow-up

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Mechanism, date, site, depth, size, airway, pain, infection, dressing, tetanus and healing are absent.
No burn classification, TBSA, treatment, referral or urgency is generated.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
